name: Zahara Reservoir - circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Algodonales offers diverse terrain at the foothills of the Sierra de Líjar and bordering the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park. The region features a compelling contrast between fertile valleys, such as the Ronda valley, and rugged white mountain ranges. Cyclists encounter varied topography, including challenging climbs and flatter stretches through agricultural areas like olive groves and vineyards. This landscape provides a mix of demanding routes and scenic paths. Best road cycling routes around Algodonales The most popular road cycling route is Zahara Reservoir - circular, a 21.5 miles (34.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a significant elevation gain of over 1,800 feet (550 meters) around the scenic Zahara Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pass of the Doves – Grazalema Village loop from Algodonales, a difficult 41.7 miles (67.0 km) path. This route includes substantial climbing, offering views of the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park and the picturesque Grazalema village. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de las Palomas – Pass of the Doves loop from Algodonales, a 38.9 miles (62.6 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Road cycling around Algodonales is defined by mountainous landscapes, fertile valleys, and agricultural areas like olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs with over 5,800 feet (1,700 meters) of elevation gain. The routes in Algodonales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Algodonales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Algodonales?
A: Algodonales offers a selection of 14 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 6 moderate and 8 difficult options available for exploration.

Q: What makes road cycling in Algodonales unique?
A: Road cycling in Algodonales is unique due to its diverse landscape, nestled at the foothills of the Sierra de Líjar and bordering the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park. You'll experience a compelling contrast between fertile valleys, such as the Ronda valley, and rugged white mountain ranges, offering both challenging climbs and scenic stretches through olive groves and vineyards.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Algodonales?
A: The routes around Algodonales are generally categorized as moderate to difficult. You'll find 6 moderate routes, which often include significant elevation gains, and 8 difficult routes, featuring substantial climbing and longer distances. This variety ensures options for both well-trained cyclists and those seeking a serious challenge.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Algodonales?
A: The road cycling routes in Algodonales are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the varied terrain, stunning views, and the challenging nature of the climbs, making it a rewarding destination for road biking.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling climbs near Algodonales?
A: Yes, Algodonales is known for its challenging climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40020098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pass of the Doves – Grazalema Village loop from Algodonales&lt;/a&gt; (67 km with 1354m elevation gain) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40017867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Palomas – Pass of the Doves loop from Algodonales&lt;/a&gt; (62.6 km with 1771m elevation gain) offer significant elevation and are popular among experienced riders seeking demanding ascents.

Q: Are there any easier or moderate road cycling routes suitable for less experienced riders?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937271901/zahara-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zahara Reservoir - circular&lt;/a&gt; route is a popular moderate choice, covering 34.6 km with 559m of elevation gain, offering a scenic ride around the reservoir without extreme difficulty.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Algodonales?
A: Yes, several road cycling routes around Algodonales are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937271901/zahara-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zahara Reservoir - circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40020098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pass of the Doves – Grazalema Village loop from Algodonales&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your ride.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes around Algodonales?
A: You can expect breathtaking and varied scenery. Routes often feature dramatic mountain backdrops of the Sierra de Líjar and Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park, lush agricultural valleys with olive groves and vineyards, and stunning views of the Zahara reservoir. The contrasting terrain provides a visually rich cycling experience.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2973761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zahara de la Sierra Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3795122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaframagón Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the Vía Verde de la Sierra. The natural beauty of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is also a highlight.

Q: Is there a dedicated cycleway or traffic-free option for road cyclists?
A: Yes, a significant attraction for cyclists is the Vía Verde de la Sierra, a 36-kilometer cycleway that follows an old railway line. While not exclusively for road bikes, it offers a traffic-free experience through tunnels and over viaducts, including the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3795122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaframagón Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, providing a unique and scenic ride.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Algodonales?
A: The best time for road cycling in Algodonales is typically during the spring and autumn months. The weather is generally milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable and enjoyable rides through the mountainous terrain.

Q: Are the roads generally quiet for cycling around Algodonales?
A: Yes, the roads further inland from the coast around Algodonales are often serene and peaceful with minimal traffic. This allows for a more immersive experience in the breathtaking scenery, making it ideal for cyclists who prefer quiet rides away from busy urban areas.

Q: Can I combine a road cycling trip with other outdoor activities in Algodonales?
A: Absolutely! Algodonales is a hub for various outdoor activities. Besides road cycling, it's internationally famous for paragliding, particularly in the Sierra de Líjar. The Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park also offers numerous opportunities for hiking and trekking, such as the pleasant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1035509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majaceite River Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: As a picturesque 'white village' with a thriving local food scene, Algodonales and the surrounding villages offer opportunities for refreshment stops. While specific cafes aren't listed for each route, you can expect to find local establishments in the villages you pass through, perfect for a break and to experience local hospitality.
tips: It's a municipality that's part of the White Villages Route. It's located in the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park, on the banks of the Zahara Reservoir.
Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir has an area of 723 ha, built in 1992. Currently, like all reservoirs in the province, it has very little water

waypoints.name: View of Zahara de la Sierra and Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir
